name: Ravi Gopinath
education: Bachelor of Engineering, Electronics and Communication from B. M. S. College of Engineering; 2nd PUC, Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, Biology from Vijaya College; High school certificate from Vijaya High School
headline: Principal Software Engineer Manager at Microsoft
about: Seasoned software engineer with over 20 years of experience in full-cycle software development, including client/server applications and large-scale online services. Proven expertise in C, C++, ASP.NET, C#, and PowerShell, with a strong grasp of the Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC). For the past 20+ years, focused on building and maintaining reliable, scalable, and well-managed online platforms. Adept at delivering high-quality solutions and driving operational excellence across diverse technical environments.

currentPositions.description:
Lead a high-performing team of engineers delivering full-stack features for SharePoint Online.

Drive design, development, and deployment of scalable solutions with a focus on quality, reliability, and performance.

Key Initiatives & Contributions:

🔹 Enterprise Content Management (ECM)

Spearheading development of ECM features that enable users to templatize and generate documents directly from Word.

Integrating advanced capabilities including form filling, AI-based deviation analysis, approval workflows, and eSignature support.

Leading product rollout through limited preview, with ongoing enhancements toward full-scale release.

🔹 Core SharePoint Features

Overseeing development and optimization of foundational SharePoint components such as Content Types, Document Sets, Document IDs, Document Parser, and Content Assembly.

Ensuring high standards of scalability, reliability, and performance across all feature areas.

Lead a high-performing team of engineers delivering full-stack features for SharePoint Online.

Drive design, development, and deployment of scalable solutions with a focus on quality, reliability, and performance.

experiences.title: Principal Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
Led efforts to enable IPv6 support for Bing, ensuring seamless functionality and parity with IPv4.

Collaborated with cross-functional teams across multiple geographies to coordinate infrastructure updates and service validation.

Ensured no loss of data or signals during the transition, maintaining consistent performance across core Bing features.

experiences.title: Senior Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
Led the initiative to enable IPv6 support for Bing, ensuring seamless access and feature parity with IPv4.

Collaborated across global teams to coordinate infrastructure updates, service validation, and deployment strategies.

Validated core Bing features—including geolocation detection, advertising, and news delivery—to ensure consistent performance under IPv6.

Played a key role in expanding Bing’s accessibility and future-proofing its network capabilities.

experiences.title: Senior Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
At Microsoft, Redmond, WA, I contributed to the design, development, deployment, and maintenance of large-scale online services using C# and .NET technologies.

Played a key role in building scalable, reliable, and high-performance features across multiple Microsoft online platforms.

Key Projects & Contributions:

🔹 SharePoint Online (SPO)

Part of initial SPO team delivering hosted SharePoint experiences.

Developed site provisioning systems for OneDrive for Business users, enhancing reliability and performance.

Led efforts in tenant provisioning, improving scalability and operational efficiency of SPO infrastructure.

🔹 OfficeLive

Developed features for OfficeLive, an online service for small businesses built on the SharePoint platform.

Implemented portal site provisioning and self-service customization capabilities for end users.

Enabled seamless onboarding and site personalization for small business clients.

🔹 bCentral

Contributed to bCentral, a suite of services for SMEs including public websites, email marketing, and contact management.

Focused on onboarding workflows and provisioning systems for new customer accounts.

🔹 Small Business Accounting (SBA)

Integrated third-party payroll services into the SBA platform, streamlining financial operations for small businesses.

experiences.title: Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
Was a contract developer at Microsoft, Redmond, WA

Developed reporting features for Business Contact Manager, an Outlook add-in for Office Small Business Edition.

Integrated Crystal Reports into the add-in, enabling users to create, customize, and view business reports.

Delivered functionality that enhanced data visibility and reporting capabilities for small business users.

experiences.title: Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
Covault Corp was a startup focused on building billing platform.

Was part of team building an online billing platform for wireless network providers using ASP.NET, C#, and SQL Server.

Implemented backend processes for importing call records and generating accurate billing statements.

Collaborated on system components across Windows and Linux environments, with additional work involving MySQL.

Contributed to building scalable and reliable billing solutions tailored for telecom service providers.

experiences.companyName: Aditi Technologies, Bangalore, India & Bellevue, USA
experiences.title: Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
Contract Software Developer – Office & Visual Studio Teams Microsoft Corporation, Redmond, WA

Contributed to development efforts across both the Office and Visual Studio teams as a contractor developer.

Visual Studio Team:

Worked with C# and ASP.NET from their alpha release, contributing to early adoption and tooling.

Designed and shipped sample applications using ASP.NET and ADO.NET for Visual Studio 1.0 Beta.

Built client and web-based applications with SQL Server backends, including setup and deployment programs.

Office Team:

Developed a migration tool in C++ to transfer clipart assets between Office versions, streamlining content portability and upgrade processes.

experiences.title: Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
Developed application software for Siemens telecom switches using VC++, MFC, and Microsoft Access.

Contributed to the design and implementation of Directory Assistance, a customer service tool used by telecom operators for efficient call handling and information retrieval.

Delivered robust and user-friendly solutions that supported high-volume telecom environments.

recommendations.name: Deb Haldar
recommendations.subtitle: Principal Group Engineering Manager at Microsoft
recommendations.context: Ravi worked with Deb on the same team
recommendations.description: I worked with Deb while he was a tester on the Auth. He was the Go-To guy on the team, and had full grasp of his domain. He has unblocked me multiple number of times with his suggestions and help. He is always willing to help out others, and rarely have I seen him say No if he knows the issue. He is technically very proficient, and understands the problem space deeply. He is an asset to any team, and would love to work with him anytime!

recommendations.name: Reed Pankhurst
recommendations.subtitle: Principal Engineering Manager at Microsoft
recommendations.context: Ravi worked with Reed but on different teams
recommendations.description: I have had Reed as a tester on one of my features, and he did an awesome job on it. He was very thorough and professional, and it was a pleasure working with him.

summary: Ravi Gopinath is currently a Microsoft professional operating within a large-enterprise product and content-services context. His posts frequently mix product announcements (notably SharePoint Syntex and Content Assembly), licensing and deployment tips, and hands-on technical perspectives about AI and machine learning for content processing and generation. He writes in a direct, practical tone that combines thought leadership, event-oriented promotion (Microsoft Ignite), and candid personal reflections on industry practices—from campus recruitment norms to consumer issues with banks. He demonstrates subject-matter expertise around content services, Syntex roadmap and adoption, and often amplifies team wins and recognitions (Forrester Wave, product rollouts). Alongside technical content, he shows community and philanthropic engagement—soliciting donations for Joy of Reading, helping colleagues find opportunities, and offering frank advice to students about international education and hiring realities. Overall his content balances technical depth and product advocacy with human-centered commentary, networking support, and occasional consumer/customer complaints.
highlights: AI & Machine Learning for Content Processing
